description: Apeirogonal tiling: infinite-sided polygons (apeirogons) meeting three at each vertex

hyperbolicity.noteIdeal: The p-vertex is an ideal point (on the boundary circle) since p=∞

shaderApproximation.note: Shader uses p=50 to approximate ideal vertex near boundary

geometry.vertexAngles: 2π/3 (three apeirogons meet at each finite vertex)
geometry.horocycles: The 'edges' of the apeirogon are geodesics from ideal points; horocycles are the level-set circles
geometry.idealVertices: The infinite 'corners' of the apeirogon lie on the boundary circle

horocycles.definition: A horocycle is a 'circle of infinite radius': it appears as a Euclidean circle internally tangent to the unit circle
horocycles.inPoincareModel: Euclidean circle tangent to unit circle from inside
horocycles.inUpperHalfPlane: Horizontal line (y = constant) or Euclidean circle tangent to real axis
horocycles.role: Horocycles are the equidistant curves from an ideal point; they tile the apeirogon strips

colorScheme: Deep ocean blues with horocycle banding, ideal-point glow
